scheme_name: Compensation for Human Death or Injury Caused by Wild Animals
page_title: Compensation for Human Death or Injury Caused by Wild Animals

details: The “Compensation for Human Death or Injury Caused by Wild Animals” scheme is launched by the Forest Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide financial relief to the families of deceased persons or individuals injured due to attacks by wild animals. The scheme covers both urban and rural areas and ensures quick assistance through a time-bound process.
benefits: ₹4,00,000 is paid to the deceased’s legal heir or family member after deducting any temporary assistance already given and the actual cost of medical treatment before death.
Actual medical expenses are reimbursed, along with ₹500 per day (maximum ₹50,000) if hospitalized, after deducting any temporary assistance already provided.
Within 3 working days in case of death.
Within 7 working days in case of injury.
The payment is made directly to the beneficiary’s bank account via e-payment.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be a victim of a wild animal attack, resulting in injury or death.
The applicant should submit proof that the injury or death was caused by a wild animal (excluding snakes, monitor lizards, and other poisonous creatures).
In the case of death, the applicant must be a legal heir, family member, or relative of the deceased person.

offline: Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal
On the homepage, click "Citizen Registration". You will be taken to the registration page.
On the registration page, fill in all the mandatory fields (name, mobile number, gender, and captcha) and click "Verify Mobile Number."
A verification code will be sent to your mobile number. Enter the code and complete the registration.
Visit the Official Website of "Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal".
Enter your User ID, Password, and Captcha Code, and click "Login."
Click on "Apply Online Service" and select the scheme you intend to apply for.

In the scheme's online application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size (fields marked with * are mandatory), and click "Submit".

faqs.question: Who can apply under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Any person or family affected by a wild animal attack, excluding snake or poisonous animal cases.

faqs.question: What is the compensation amount for death cases?
faqs.answer: ₹4,00,000 after adjusting temporary assistance and medical expenses.

faqs.question: What is the compensation for injury cases?
faqs.answer: Actual treatment expenses and ₹500 per day (up to ₹50,000) if hospitalized.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee?
faqs.answer: No, the application process is free of cost.

faqs.question: Where can I apply?
faqs.answer: At the nearest Forest Range Office or online at mpedistrict.gov.in

faqs.question: What is the time limit for compensation in death cases?
What is the time limit for compensation in injury cases?
Are urban residents eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, both urban and rural residents are eligible.

faqs.question: How is the payment made?
faqs.answer: Payment is made directly to the beneficiary’s bank account through e-payment.

faqs.question: What happens if the application is rejected?
faqs.answer: The applicant can file an appeal within 30 days to the Divisional Forest Officer.

faqs.question: Can I appeal again if not satisfied with the first appeal?
faqs.answer: Yes, a second appeal can be made to the Conservator of Forests within 60 days.

faqs.question: Which wild animal attacks are not covered?
faqs.answer: Attacks by snakes, monitor lizards, and other poisonous animals.
